(ACCC) is a [fundamentalist](/wiki/Christian_fundamentalism "Christian fundamentalism") organization set up in opposition to the Federal Council of Churches (now [National Council of Churches](/wiki/National_Council_of_Churches "National Council of Churches")).
["What is the Difference Between the American Council of Christian Churches and the National Council of the Churches of Christ in the U.S.A.?"](http://www.carlmcintire.org/booklets-acccVnccc.php).

[ACCC responds to *proper Church* comment](http://www.amcouncilcc.org/ProperChurchComment.pdf) [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20090509033538/http://www.amcouncilcc.org/ProperChurchComment.pdf) 2009-05-09 at the [Wayback Machine](/wiki/Wayback_Machine "Wayback Machine")

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

The **American Council of Christian Churches** (ACCC) is a [fundamentalist](/wiki/Christian_fundamentalism "Christian fundamentalism") organization set up in opposition to the Federal Council of Churches (now [National Council of Churches](/wiki/National_Council_of_Churches "National Council of Churches")).

The council's motto is Jude 3, "Earnestly contending for the Faith".

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=American_Council_of_Christian_Churches&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

ACCC was founded in 1941 under the leadership of [Carl McIntire](/wiki/Carl_McIntire "Carl McIntire").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

Membership in the American Council of Christian Churches is available to denominations and individual Christians, who are admitted by a 3/4 majority vote. Agreement with the purposes and doctrinal statement are required and membership is specifically denied to those who have affiliations with

> the [World Council of Churches](/wiki/World_Council_of_Churches "World Council of Churches") (WCC) or any of its affiliates, such as the [National Council of the Churches of Christ in the U.S.A.](/wiki/National_Council_of_Churches "National Council of Churches") (NCC), the [World Evangelical Fellowship](/wiki/World_Evangelical_Fellowship "World Evangelical Fellowship") (WEF) or any of its affiliates, such as the [National Association of Evangelicals](/wiki/National_Association_of_Evangelicals "National Association of Evangelicals") (NAE), the modern Charismatic Movement, or the [Ecumenical Movement](/wiki/Christian_ecumenism "Christian ecumenism")...

The ACCC has remained small in comparison to the [National Association of Evangelicals](/wiki/National_Association_of_Evangelicals "National Association of Evangelicals") and the [National Council of Churches](/wiki/National_Council_of_Churches "National Council of Churches"). This is due in part to its strong [separatist](/wiki/Ecclesiastical_separatism "Ecclesiastical separatism") stance, and in part because separatist denominations will often not participate in "cross-denominational" organizations.

Dan Greenfield currently serves as Executive Secretary. Offices are in [Orwell, Ohio.](/wiki/Bethlehem,_Pennsylvania "Bethlehem, Pennsylvania")

In July 2007, the leadership of the ACCC issued a statement criticizing a recent declaration by the [Congregation for the Doctrine of the Faith](/wiki/Congregation_for_the_Doctrine_of_the_Faith "Congregation for the Doctrine of the Faith") on the meaning of the phrase *[subsistit in](/wiki/Subsistit_in "Subsistit in")* which re-emphasized the role of the Catholic Church as the subsisting Church of Christ.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Members – April 2019

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=American_Council_of_Christian_Churches&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Members – April 2019")]

* Appalachian Independent Minister’s Fellowship
* Association of Ministers of the Reformed Faith
* Faith Presbytery, Bible Presbyterian Church
* [Evangelical Methodist Church of America](/wiki/Evangelical_Methodist_Church "Evangelical Methodist Church")
* [Fellowship of Fundamental Bible Churches](/wiki/Fellowship_of_Fundamental_Bible_Churches "Fellowship of Fundamental Bible Churches")
* [Free Presbyterian Church of North America](/wiki/Free_Presbyterian_Church_of_North_America "Free Presbyterian Church of North America")
* [Fundamental Methodist Church](/wiki/Fundamental_Methodist_Church "Fundamental Methodist Church")
* [Independent Baptist Fellowship of North America](/wiki/Independent_Baptist_Fellowship_of_North_America "Independent Baptist Fellowship of North America")
* Independent Churches Affiliated

## Presidents

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=American_Council_of_Christian_Churches&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Presidents")]

* Carl McIntire
* Robert T. Ketcham
* William Wallace Breckbill
* [Clyde J. Kennedy](/wiki/Clyde_J._Kennedy "Clyde J. Kennedy") (1958–1961)
* Don McKnight
* Richard Harris
* L. Duane Brown
* E. Allen Griffith
* John McKnight
* David Mook
* Jonathan Smith
